steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: ‘the source of a claim is _____: it has no direct bearing on whether the claim is true.’ The colon defines the blank — the word must mean ‘having no direct bearing on whether the claim is true,’ i.e., not relevant to the matter at hand.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — implicit: means implied or unstated. The source of a claim is usually known, not implied, so this does not match the colon definition. ✗
  • Choice B — indisputable: means unquestionable. The passage is about whether the source affects the truth of the claim, not about whether the source itself is beyond dispute. ✗
  • Choice C — indistinct: means unclear or vague. The passage says nothing about the source being hard to identify — the source is known; it just doesn’t affect truth. ✗
  • Choice D — irrelevant: means having no bearing on the matter. This exactly matches the colon’s definition: ‘it has no direct bearing on whether the claim is true.’ ✓
That matches Choice D — irrelevant.
